reason: The score's core premise is flawed. Sir Cadogan was cut entirely from the theatrical release of Prisoner of Azkaban - his scene only exists as a deleted scene on home media. The justifications repeatedly reference what 'the film' shows (password incident, introductory scene, Neville being implicated) as if these are in the theatrical cut, but they are not. A character with no theatrical presence should score far lower, particularly on narrative_role (15/25 seems too generous) and arc (13/25). The personality description of his book traits is accurate, but the film comparison is built on deleted footage, which fundamentally undermines the scoring basis.
